questions.question: What is one of the natural wonders described in the chapter?
Where is the Valley of Flowers located?
questions.answer: In the Chamoli district of Uttarakhand.

questions.question: Why is the Valley of Flowers famous?
questions.answer: Because of its colourful flowers and biodiversity.

questions.question: What type of environment surrounds the Valley of Flowers?
What does biodiversity mean?
questions.answer: The variety of living organisms in an ecosystem.

questions.question: Name another natural wonder mentioned in the chapter.
Where are Living Root Bridges found?
From which tree are Living Root Bridges formed?
questions.answer: From the roots of rubber trees.

questions.question: Who creates Living Root Bridges?
Why do Living Root Bridges become stronger over time?
questions.answer: Because the tree roots continue to grow and thicken.

questions.question: What natural features add beauty to the Valley of Flowers?
Which organization recognizes important natural sites like the Valley of Flowers?
Why is biodiversity important in natural ecosystems?
Name one animal found in the Valley of Flowers ecosystem.
What does the term ecosystem mean?
questions.answer: The interaction between living organisms and their environment.

questions.question: Why are natural wonders important to protect?
questions.answer: To preserve biodiversity and natural ecosystems.

questions.question: Name one factor that can harm natural wonders.
What type of tourism helps protect natural environments?
How do Living Root Bridges show harmony with nature?
questions.answer: They use natural tree roots without harming the environment.

questions.question: What inspires people to respect nature in the chapter?
What role do local communities play in conservation?
questions.answer: They help protect and preserve natural environments.

questions.question: Which climate supports the growth of flowers in the valley?
What message does the chapter give about nature?
questions.answer: Nature should be protected and respected.

questions.question: What value does the chapter emphasize?
What is the main idea of the chapter?
questions.answer: Natural beauty and environmental protection go together.
